description: Interactive tool that teaches handpicking as a method of separation: pulling out a few odd, easy-to-spot impurities (grey pebbles) from a heap of golden wheat grains, and discovering hands-on why handpicking stops being convenient once the pile gets large. Built for Class 6 Science (NCERT Curiosity, Chapter 9: Methods of Separation of Substances).

curriculum.subject: Science
curriculum.chapter: 9. Methods of Separation in Everyday Life
learningObjectives: Define handpicking as separating unwanted bits from a mixture by hand.
Recognise that handpicking works when impurities differ in size or colour and are few in number.
Experience why handpicking becomes slow and inconvenient for large quantities.
Connect to faster methods (winnowing, sieving) used for bigger piles.
Spot handpicking in everyday life (cleaning dal/rice, sorting produce, etc.).

render.styling: css-in-js (inline styles + injected @keyframes, no Tailwind, no external CSS)
render.notes: Single-file component. Injects an #hp-keyframes <style> block on mount and removes it on unmount. setStepDetails is called once per mode change with { currentStep: 1, totalSteps: 1, isPaused: true, currentMode }. This tool is interaction-driven, not an auto-advancing step sequence.

width.description: Maximum width of the tool shell in pixels. The component is responsive and flows to content height.

height.description: Accepted by the props interface but not used for layout — the shell auto-sizes to its content. Reserved for forward compatibility.

initialMode.description: Which mode is active when the tool first renders.

showModeSelector.description: Show or hide the row of mode tabs (Learn / Pick It / Real World / Free Pile). Hide it to lock the student into a single mode.

enabledModes.description: Which modes appear as tabs. Order is preserved. Combine with initialMode to focus the experience.

showNavigation.description: Show the Back / Next buttons on the Learn and Real World card walks.

showStepIndicator.description: Show the clickable progress dots beneath the Learn and Real World cards.

animationSpeed.description: Animation speed multiplier. >1 speeds up transitions and the pebble-to-bowl flight; <1 slows them down.

themeColor.description: Primary accent (hex). Used for eyebrows, the Learn card accent, and the glow/keyframe tint. The wheat/earthy palette is intentional for this concept.

darkMode.description: Switch the shell to a dark, warm-toned surface.

unsupportedParameters.description: These appear in the reference template but are intentionally NOT implemented here. Passing them has no effect.

additionalProps.description: Tool-specific dynamic content the agent can pass to tune the piles, colours, and copy.

round1Pebbles.description: Number of pebbles in the small Round 1 pile (Practice mode).

round1Grains.description: Number of wheat grains in the Round 1 pile.

round2Pebbles.description: Number of pebbles in the large Round 2 pile (the 'big pile' that drives the lesson).

round2Grains.description: Number of wheat grains in the Round 2 pile.

maxPileGrains.description: Upper limit of the wheat-grains slider in Free Pile (Hands On) mode.

maxPilePebbles.description: Upper limit of the pebbles slider in Free Pile (Hands On) mode.

thaliColor.description: Base colour of the round wooden tray (thali). Lightened/darkened automatically for shading.

grainColor.description: Fallback wheat-grain colour. Grains also cycle through three golden tints for variety.

pebbleColor.description: Fallback pebble colour. Pebbles also cycle through three grey tints.

bowlColor.description: Colour of the collection bowl that picked pebbles fly into.

hintText.default: That is a wheat grain — small, golden and oval with a groove down the middle. Pebbles are larger, dull grey and round.
hintText.description: Hint shown when the student taps a wheat grain by mistake. Reinforces the size/colour difference that makes handpicking possible.

showTimer.description: Show the live timer stat while picking. Turn off to remove time pressure.

promptAfterRound1.default: Quick and easy with just a few pebbles. But what if the heap were much bigger?
promptAfterRound1.description: Bridging prompt shown on the Round 1 result card, before the student loads the bigger pile.

exaggerate_the_big_pile.promptAfterRound1: Easy, right? Now imagine a whole sack at the mill...

learn.label: Learn
learn.description: Four concept cards: what handpicking is, when it works, why it gets slow, and what comes next.

practice.label: Pick It
practice.description: Round 1 (small pile) → result with time/pebble/mis-tap stats → load the bigger pile → Round 2 → reflection comparing the two and pointing to winnowing/sieving.

real_world.label: Real World
real_world.description: Four everyday examples of handpicking: cleaning dal & rice, sorting good from spoiled grain, grading produce, and pulling a wilted leaf from salad.

hands_on.label: Free Pile
hands_on.description: Free play: two sliders set the number of grains and pebbles, then the student times themselves clearing the pile and watches the time climb as pebbles increase.

agentGuide.overview: Use this tool to teach handpicking. Learn and Real World are read-and-tap card walks; Pick It (Practice) is the core interactive — students pick grey pebbles out of golden wheat, first from a small pile then a large one, and feel why it gets slow; Free Pile lets them vary the pile themselves. Pebbles are larger and dull grey; wheat grains are smaller, golden, oval with a groove — tapping a grain by mistake triggers a hint.

quickReference.studentRequest: Why can't we just handpick everything?
quickReference.use: initialMode: "practice" (let them feel Round 2), optionally exaggerate via additionalProps.round2Pebbles

quickReference.studentRequest: Where is it used in real life?
Let me build my own pile

visibilityControl.showModeSelector: Hide the tabs to lock a single mode.
visibilityControl.showNavigation: Hide Back/Next on card walks (dots can still navigate if showStepIndicator is true).
visibilityControl.showStepIndicator: Hide the progress dots on card walks.

toolCallExamples.name: Intro the concept
toolCallExamples.title: What is Handpicking?
toolCallExamples.instructions_for_student: Read through the cards to learn what handpicking is and when it works.

toolCallExamples.name: Hands-on pick (small then big pile)
toolCallExamples.title: Pick the Pebbles
toolCallExamples.instructions_for_student: Tap out every grey pebble. Then try the bigger pile and notice how much longer it takes!

toolCallExamples.name: Drive home 'why it gets slow'
toolCallExamples.title: When Handpicking Gets Hard

additionalProps.promptAfterRound1: Easy with a few. Now imagine a whole sack at the mill...

toolCallExamples.instructions_for_student: Clear the small pile, then the big one. Why do farmers use winnowing and sieving for huge heaps?

toolCallExamples.name: Real-world only
toolCallExamples.title: Handpicking Around You
toolCallExamples.instructions_for_student: Swipe through everyday places where we separate things just by hand.

toolCallExamples.name: Free exploration
toolCallExamples.title: Build Your Own Pile
toolCallExamples.instructions_for_student: Set the grains and pebbles, then time yourself. Push the pebbles up and watch the clock!

decisionTree: Student asks for a definition/explanation -> initialMode: learn
Student wants to do/play -> initialMode: practice
Goal is the 'large quantity is inconvenient' insight -> initialMode: practice, raise round2Pebbles/round2Grains
Student asks for examples/uses -> initialMode: real_world
Student wants open exploration -> initialMode: hands_on
Need a single focused activity -> set enabledModes to one mode and showModeSelector: false
tips: The pedagogical payoff is the contrast between Round 1 and Round 2 in Practice — keep Round 1 small and Round 2 clearly larger.
Don't pass showPlayPause / filterSteps / initialStep / autoPlayDuration; they're not implemented here.
Keep round counts reasonable (hundreds of grains) — extremely large piles add many DOM nodes and can feel sluggish on low-end devices.
Use hintText to localise or re-word the size/colour cue if needed.
errorHandling: If pebble counts are set to 0, the round can never complete; always keep round1Pebbles and round2Pebbles >= 1.
Slider values are clamped to [min, maxPile*]; values above the max are not selectable in Free Pile.
Invalid initialMode falls back to rendering the learn card walk.
